/rules/adventure/advancement.title: Advancement & Leveling Up
/rules/adventure/advancement.preview: Gaining a Level provides the following benefits:

/rules/adventure/conditions.title: Conditions
/rules/adventure/conditions.preview: See the full list of conditions at the [[conditions|Conditions Index]].

/rules/adventure/digging-excavation.title: Digging & Excavation
/rules/adventure/digging-excavation.preview: Characters can move cubic feet of earth equal to their Max. Load in # per hour, assuming appropriate tools are possessed. The rate is halved for improvised tools, and quartered for a lack of them.…

/rules/adventure/encumbrance.title: Encumbrance
/rules/adventure/encumbrance.preview: 10lb is **1 stone** (**1#**). Everything is weighed in stone.

/rules/adventure/foresight.title: Foresight
/rules/adventure/foresight.preview: PCs are generally assumed to be competent & to also notice a variety of details & niche planning that their players may not have had the time or focus to realize. For this reason, when presented with…

/rules/adventure/hunting.title: Hunting Rare Beasts
/rules/adventure/hunting.preview: The Move: Hunting (p. @) covers the mechanical procedure for organized hunts. The rules below cover the procedure for individual or small-party wilderness hunting.

/rules/adventure/movement.title: Movement
/rules/adventure/movement.preview: Stairs & hills steeper than 45 degrees are **Rough Terrain** & halve your Run score. The GM may also rule certain other kinds of terrain (such as tightropes, which may require a Save to cross…

/rules/adventure/negotiation-persuasion.title: Negotiation & Persuasion
/rules/adventure/negotiation-persuasion.preview: In *strictly* mechanical terms, NPCs can *only* be **Friendly**, **Neutral**, or **Hostile** towards any given actor, including Factions such as the Party, or individual PCs. You don't have to write…

/rules/adventure/resting.title: Resting
/rules/adventure/resting.preview: **Short Rest**: A brief pause of up to an hour. Restores no HP but allows characters to catch their breath, bind minor wounds, eat, and prepare spells or Totems.

/rules/adventure/travel.title: (Move) Travel
/rules/adventure/travel.preview: Travel by a Party or mobile Factions (such as Armies) is resolved via *free movement* tracked on a map of the campaign world by the GM.

/rules/armies/army-proficiency.title: Army Proficiency
/rules/armies/army-proficiency.preview: Armies have a **proficiency** ranking; they can be **green**, **veteran**, or **elite**.

/rules/armies/axioms.title: Axioms
/rules/armies/axioms.preview: **Aia is a flat discworld & the implications of this has a radical impact on military operations** which renders the entirety of the real world's strategic & tactical doctrine incomplete, misleading,…

/rules/armies/cultural-retinues.title: Cultural Retinues
/rules/armies/cultural-retinues.preview: A **culture** in the context of war is a distinct military tradition shaped by a region's social structure, strategic doctrine, technological capability, demographics & economic infrastructure that…

/rules/armies/personal-retinues.title: Personal Retinues
/rules/armies/personal-retinues.preview: You can build your own army by paying the cost for *equipment* & *training*. The cost is approximately $1,000 plus 1 MP Debt per soldier, with a training time of 1 month for green conscripts. It is…

/rules/armies/raising-armies.title: Raising Armies
/rules/armies/raising-armies.preview: You can raise armies in 3 ways.

/rules/character-creation/classes/burghe.title: Burgher
/rules/character-creation/classes/burghe.preview: What is a sword? Paid for by debt, a creator of debt — for every man slain by it is a debt in blood, owed to his wife, his children & his clan. And a debt is not easily forgotten; men will gain vast…

/rules/character-creation/classes/gangst.title: Gangster
/rules/character-creation/classes/gangst.preview: You are a smuggler, racketeer, & underworld financier. You *solve problems* beyond the scope of what the Lords' courts can help with. You enforce contracts & loans a Magistrate would arrest both…

/rules/character-creation/classes/lord.title: Lord
/rules/character-creation/classes/lord.preview: You have a porcelain mask. It covers half your face. It is traced with ruby & jade & sweeping gilded lines. You were given one at birth & have worn one since that day.

/rules/character-creation/classes/magici.title: Magician
/rules/character-creation/classes/magici.preview: **Magicians are unstable**. Magic is horrifically dangerous. Few magicians live without suffering mutations, personal injury, and an eventual horrifying death. Sorcerer-kings are feared. Tame…

/rules/character-creation/classes/magist.title: Magistrate
/rules/character-creation/classes/magist.preview: You have a seal. It is carved from jade, set in bronze, & it bears your name and office in the old Imperial script. Without it, you are nothing. With it, you are the law.

/rules/character-creation/classes/warlor.title: Warlord
/rules/character-creation/classes/warlor.preview: "Mercenary" is such a *dishonorable* word. A good mercenary *keeps* his word. A contract is your life-line; clients won't contract you if you don't pay heed to past promises.

/rules/character-creation/classes.title: Classes
/rules/character-creation/classes.preview: Your **Class** is your literal social position in Glass. It determines what you can become and which pools of power you can draw from.

/rules/character-creation/level-retainer.title: Level & Retainer
/rules/character-creation/level-retainer.preview: Your **Level** in Glass Empires is derived from *the people who depend upon & support you*. Your "Level" is *borrowed* from your *largest* ruled [[factions|Faction]] — specifically, it's determined by…

/rules/character-creation/magical-tradit.title: Forester
/rules/character-creation/magical-tradit.preview: The natural world is chaotic & disordered. Nature is the enemy of civilization. Men are born into a world that wishes them dead. Were it the choice of nature, the mortal tribes would struggle under…

/rules/character-creation/magical-tradit.title: Jester
/rules/character-creation/magical-tradit.preview: A clown is a warning. An excess of fear, of curiosity, of sloth, of disregard. Clowns are *excessive*. They warn through example.

/rules/character-creation/magical-tradit.title: Metallurge
/rules/character-creation/magical-tradit.preview: Metal wants to usurp. It is metal that let men dishonor the bear, sever sinew, cut stone.

/rules/character-creation/magical-tradit.title: Origami Assassin
/rules/character-creation/magical-tradit.preview: A tool does not carve its own path. It is wielded by the hand that grips it. A blank sheet is an infinite canvas. Folded properly, it makes a million masks.

/rules/character-creation/magical-tradit.title: Snowmime
/rules/character-creation/magical-tradit.preview: Out in the taiga, between the mountain's towering rock-pillars & beneath boughs of icy conifer, silence deafens the senses. A blanket of snow is a quieting thing. It cushions sound, smothers it as it…

/rules/character-creation/magical-tradit.title: Telluric Priest
/rules/character-creation/magical-tradit.preview: The salamanders' world is split in two, & the halves are necessary, complementary & harmonious. Qali reigns in heaven as sky, overseeing ten thousand stellar patriarchs; Impa rests beneath as earth,…

/rules/character-creation/magical-tradit.title: Magical Traditions
/rules/character-creation/magical-tradit.preview: Every [[magician|magician]] has a **Magical Tradition** from which their specialization descends. Your Tradition is your philosophy & history that shapes the way you see the world & the sort of…

/rules/character-creation/origins.title: Origins
/rules/character-creation/origins.preview: Roll d66 twice on either of the two origin tables. For each roll, you may choose whether to roll on Table A or Table B. For each result, write a Fact with this formula: "*Because I [was (a)…], I…

/rules/character-creation/party-cooperat.title: Party Cooperation
/rules/character-creation/party-cooperat.preview: Glass Empires can be played either as a PvP Braunstein or as a PvE Party-based campaign. If you're playing a game where all Players are part of a single cooperative Party, this step is mandatory;…

/rules/character-creation/race.title: Race
/rules/character-creation/race.preview: Your **Race** is your lineage. You may pick any Race that your Class is eligible for. Your character inherits the Race's *statblock* (write down each stat on your character sheet) as well as its…

/rules/character-creation/races/coraler.title: Coraler
/rules/character-creation/races/coraler.preview: *@ tbh this needs a rewrite. too poetic & not relevant enough to Glass's context.*

/rules/character-creation/races/harpy.title: Harpy
/rules/character-creation/races/harpy.preview: Harpies have a bad rap. It's not that flying isn't useful — they're favored as messengers & scout by everyone. But when you can't manage an ounce of foresight & your principal unit of society is *an…

/rules/character-creation/races/human.title: Human
/rules/character-creation/races/human.preview: Glass — the rivers run with ruby-crusted sands! Glass — the water-flows off arching rooftop gutter channels! Glass — gold-white birches paint the land!

/rules/character-creation/races/salamand.title: Salamander
/rules/character-creation/races/salamand.preview: There are no records of dwarves. No faces, no records in the Templevaults they were said to have built. After their supposed vanishing off the face of Aia, the salamanders are their only testament — a…

/rules/classes.title: Classes
/rules/classes.preview: Your **Class** is your literal social position in Glass. It determines what you can become and which pools of power you can draw from.

/rules/combat/armor.title: Armor & Armor Points
/rules/combat/armor.preview: Armor reduces incoming Physical damage by its **Armor value**. A character's Armor value is determined by the best armor they are wearing.

/rules/combat/battle-conditions.title: Battle Conditions
/rules/combat/battle-conditions.preview: **Concentration** is a Condition for characters who are preoccupied with an ongoing activity & cannot focus clearly. Concentration ends if a character takes any amount of Damage or falls under a…

/rules/combat/battle-procedure.title: Battle Procedure
/rules/combat/battle-procedure.preview: A **Unit** is a single character, or a Group (p. @) of statistically equivalent characters, representing a discrete unit of command on the battlefield.

/rules/combat/battle-rating.title: Battle Rating
/rules/combat/battle-rating.preview: **Battle Rating** (BR) is a score representing everything that makes a character uniquely competent in a general combat scenario, not including exceptional cases. The formula for BR is as follows:

/rules/combat/battles.title: Battles
/rules/combat/battles.preview: **Battles** are intended for large-scale battles held between *any* number of individuals (but especially any battle above 12 combatants) where *speed* is more important than *detail*.

/rules/combat/damage-death.title: Damage, Incapacitation & Death
/rules/combat/damage-death.preview: When a character takes damage, subtract it from their current HP. Damage values are flat numbers — Glass Empires does not roll for damage. Variance comes from the Save system: a Crit doubles damage, a…

/rules/combat/damage-types.title: Damage Types
/rules/combat/damage-types.preview: Glass Empires has exactly seven damage types. No Slashing, Piercing, Bleed, Cold (use Ice), Radiant, Necrotic, Sonic, Force, or Psychic (use Mind). Multi-type damage uses the target's weakest…

/rules/combat/end-of-battle.title: End of Battle
/rules/combat/end-of-battle.preview: When all members of one or both sides are defeated, or if a Round has ended where one side was fleeing, the battle is over. Fleeing sides count as defeated.

/rules/combat/engagement.title: Engagement
/rules/combat/engagement.preview: When you enter an enemy's reach, you become **Engaged** with them. Not as in marriage; rather, if you attempt to *leave* their reach without first taking a Disengage Action (p. @), they may perform a…

/rules/combat/flight-pursuit.title: Flight & Pursuit
/rules/combat/flight-pursuit.preview: Flight & pursuit is played out as a single Round of Battle (p. @) between all pursued & pursuers.

/rules/combat/funerals.title: Funerals
/rules/combat/funerals.preview: See also: [[using-corpses-spirits|Using Corpses & Spirits]] for other ways to handle a corpse.

/rules/combat/mounted-combat.title: Mounted Combat
/rules/combat/mounted-combat.preview: Mounts are *combatants* if they're either sapient or carnivorous. Otherwise, they're noncombatants. The following adjustments apply for mounts & their riders:

/rules/combat/movement-space.title: Movement & Space
/rules/combat/movement-space.preview: Glass Empires by default uses a *gridless* approach to combat.

/rules/combat/panic-fear.title: Panic & Fear
/rules/combat/panic-fear.preview: Characters Save to avoid Panic (synonymous with terror, fear, etc.) when any of the following happens:

/rules/combat/skirmish-actions.title: Skirmish Actions
/rules/combat/skirmish-actions.preview: Skirmish Actions are Moves that take a very brief amount of time to resolve. These Moves can be used outside of Skirmishes to resolve what can be accomplished with short-term action durations —…

/rules/combat/skirmishes.title: Skirmishes
/rules/combat/skirmishes.preview: **Skirmishes** are intended for detailed or intricate fast-paced combat between a small number of relatively powerful individuals in a constrained combat area. They take longer to resolve than Battles…

/rules/creation-process.title: Creation Process
/rules/creation-process.preview: This procedure creates a Player Character embedded in Glass's factional landscape. New characters begin as leaders of small Factions with a Follower or two, some resources, and the scars of their…

/rules/factions/faction-moves.title: Faction Moves
/rules/factions/faction-moves.preview: A Leader can orchestrate one of their Factions into performing a Move with them, such as Resting (p. @) an Army or stockpiling assets (p. @) using the Faction's collective labor force. Many Moves are…

/rules/factions/faction-resolution.title: Faction Move Resolution
/rules/factions/faction-resolution.preview: Whenever the Deadline for a Move made by a Faction comes up on the calendar (p. @), run through the procedure below.

/rules/factions/faction-types.title: Faction Types
/rules/factions/faction-types.preview: **A Faction has exactly one Type**. Nobody can be good at everything, & every institution has its own weaknesses reflected in its intrinsic character.

/rules/factions/families-capitals.title: Families, Capitals & Settlements
/rules/factions/families-capitals.preview: **Families** is a score indicating the *number of households* located in a Faction's **Capital** that provide *support for* & *has a dependency* on that Faction. *All* human power is downstream from…

/rules/factions/growing-settlement.title: Growing a Settlement
/rules/factions/growing-settlement.preview: In order to *grow* a Settlement to the minimum required Families for its Ruler to gain a Level, the following conditions must be true:

/rules/factions/hidden-factions.title: Hidden Factions
/rules/factions/hidden-factions.preview: A *hidden* Faction is trying to hide itself from others, or from a specific Faction. It may only be trying to hide a specific secret (such as that it's actually the Subfaction of a different Faction).…

/rules/factions/move-capital-relocation.title: (Move) Capital Relocation
/rules/factions/move-capital-relocation.preview: You can relocate your Capital to a new Settlement or Location. The old Capital becomes a provincial seat or secondary holding; the new one becomes the administrative heart of your Faction.

/rules/factions/move-create-subfaction.title: (Move) Create Subfaction
/rules/factions/move-create-subfaction.preview: To create a new Subfaction, you must:

/rules/factions/move-faction-activity.title: (Move) Faction Activity
/rules/factions/move-faction-activity.preview: Lots of weird Faction actions won't cleanly fall into any other niche. In these cases, a GM may determine a **Deadline** based on the nature of the act & use the Success Save template table below to…

/rules/factions/move-faction-sabotage.title: (Move) Sabotage
/rules/factions/move-faction-sabotage.preview: Intangible forms of harm — trade deals, subterfuge, espionage, debt, political & religious conflict, & other forms of intangible & indirect conflict — are handled via this Move. To commit Sabotage,…

/rules/factions/move-grow-followers.title: (Move) Grow Follower Base
/rules/factions/move-grow-followers.preview: If your Faction has its Capital located *inside* a Settlement & only has Families equal to a *portion* of that Settlement's total Families, you may attempt to raise your number of Families by…

/rules/factions/move-make-asset.title: (Move) Make Asset
/rules/factions/move-make-asset.preview: Make a non-commodity physical asset, such as a statue, building, fortress, bridge, or road.

/rules/factions/move-plunder.title: (Move) Plunder a Settlement
/rules/factions/move-plunder.preview: To plunder a controlled Settlement, you must order an Army whose BR is at least equal to the Settlement's Families to do so.

/rules/factions/party-is-faction.title: The Party is a Faction
/rules/factions/party-is-faction.preview: But only if the PCs make it one.

/rules/factions/resources.title: Resources
/rules/factions/resources.preview: **Placeholder**: This section previously lived under the old Adventure chapter and will be expanded here with rules for faction resources, asset management, and economic factors.

/rules/factions/sovereignty-subfactions.title: Sovereignty & Subfactions
/rules/factions/sovereignty-subfactions.preview: Many of a Faction's Actions are delegated to **Subfactions**, which are **Subordinate** Factions. Each Subfaction is led by its own Leader, who is a **Retainer** of their **Liege** (the Leader of the…

/rules/factions/type-army.title: @ Army
/rules/factions/type-army.preview: Your Capital is a *mobile encampment*. This covers nomads, merchant caravans, rogue mercenary companies, armies, and other itinerant Factions. What gets created when you raise an Army.

/rules/factions/type-business.title: Business
/rules/factions/type-business.preview: Your Capital is a *business*, a mercantile or artisan venture.

/rules/factions/type-government.title: Government
/rules/factions/type-government.preview: Your Capital is a *Settlement*. You rule the *entire* Settlement as its formal governmental authority, whether that be as a monarchy, oligarchic mayoral tenure, or republic. Your income is *tax money*…

/rules/factions/type-mine-clan.title: Mine Clan
/rules/factions/type-mine-clan.preview: Your Capital is a *mine*, an underground center for resource production. In Glass, *every* Mine is managed by Salamanders; Glassians no longer know how. Old restrictions on underground housing have…

/rules/introduction/campaigns.title: Campaigns
/rules/introduction/campaigns.preview: A campaign consists of a **calendar** (p. @) & a **map** depicting the world or a region of the world in which the game takes place.

/rules/introduction/dice-notation.title: Dice & Notation
/rules/introduction/dice-notation.preview: Glass Empires uses exclusively six-sided dice. We call them d6 dice, where 6 indicates their number of sides. While other systems often use d4, d8, d10, or other polyhedral dice,  **d4, d6, d8, d10,…

/rules/introduction/facts.title: Facts
/rules/introduction/facts.preview: A **Fact** is a compact, true statement about a character (a creature, PC, NPC, or Faction) that has mechanical weight. Facts are the game's replacement for traditional ability scores (Strength,…

/rules/introduction/money.title: Money
/rules/introduction/money.preview: A **silver piece** ($1) represents one day of unskilled labor & is the smallest denomination of coin in circulation. Most things are paid for with debt (p. @), typically via the gift economy. An…

/rules/introduction/saves.title: Saves
/rules/introduction/saves.preview: A **Save** is a standardized 2d6 dice roll made to determine the effect of actions whose outcome is both uncertain & meaningful.

/rules/magic/divination.title: Divination
/rules/magic/divination.preview: **There is no difference between a god & a creature**. A god is a power differential. A king is a god to a peasant. Most "gods" are exceptionally weird creatures or powerful people who, through guile,…

/rules/magic/extracting-spirits.title: Extracting Spirits
/rules/magic/extracting-spirits.preview: Anyone whose Facts/background indicates they learned *magic* (as spirits are intrinsic to all magic) can extract a spirit. Doing so takes 1 hour per corpse HD & requires you have a viable Totem on…

/rules/magic/move-casting.title: (Move) Casting
/rules/magic/move-casting.preview: **Casting** is a Major Action. There are two casting methods to follow depending on the *kind* of spirit you have. There are two kinds of spell-spirits:

/rules/magic/ritual-cooking.title: Ritual Cooking
/rules/magic/ritual-cooking.preview: Anyone whose Facts/background indicates they learned *cooking* can make **food** out of a fresh spirited corpse. Such food is said to convey some of the blessings — & horrors — of a given creature.

/rules/magic/spell-dice.title: Spell Dice
/rules/magic/spell-dice.preview: **Spell Dice** (henceforth **SD**) are a resource gained by magicians representing their increased familiarity with & capability for handling spirits. Each represents a d6 die.

/rules/magic/totems.title: Totems
/rules/magic/totems.preview: A **Totem** is a trinket made out of dead Wood or Flesh (the elements; such as bone or timber) that can hold a single spirit (**spell** or **soul**) for spellcasting or other purposes. They come in…

/rules/magic/using-corpses-spirits.title: Using Corpses & Spirits
/rules/magic/using-corpses-spirits.preview: A spirit in a newly-dead corpse can be manipulated in a number of useful ways.

/rules/marketplace/construction-projects.title: Construction & Projects
/rules/marketplace/construction-projects.preview: Rules for construction projects, fortifications, infrastructure, and public works.

/rules/marketplace/debt-credit.title: Debt & Credit
/rules/marketplace/debt-credit.preview: A **Debt** is a Condition (a temporary Fact) assigned a Value, whether monetary or intangible (i.e. blood debts, "he murdered my relative"). It represents what you owe to a creditor — the person you…

/rules/marketplace/equipment.title: Equipment
/rules/marketplace/equipment.preview: If it's necessary to determine the quantity of an item that can be bought in a given Settlement, divide 5 times the Settlement's Families by the item's Value. The result, rounded down, is how many can…

/rules/marketplace/on-prices-and-power.title: On Prices & Power
/rules/marketplace/on-prices-and-power.preview: Most people interact with money as discrete volumes that are received & spent. You are paid so much silver per day; you spend it on discrete objects. This behavior makes sense if & only if you are not…

/rules/marketplace/retainers-hirelings.title: Retainers & Hirelings
/rules/marketplace/retainers-hirelings.preview: To find out how many characters of a certain Level (# of Class Features) are available for hire in a Settlement per season, use the table below.

/rules/moves.title: Moves
/rules/moves.preview: A **Move** is any action taken in-game by an actor. Moves can be long or short-term actions; they may involve a chance of failure (& if they do, probably invoke a Save). They may involve movement,…

/rules/races.title: Races
/rules/races.preview: Your **Race** is your lineage. You may pick any Race that your Class is eligible for. Your character inherits the Race's *statblock* (write down each stat on your character sheet) as well as its…

/rules/what-is-a-faction.title: What Is a Faction
/rules/what-is-a-faction.preview: A **Faction** is one or more characters that compose a set of dependent & supportive **Families** surrounding a place of operations called a **Capital**, working under a notable or charismatic…

/rules/what-this-game-is.title: What This Game Is
/rules/what-this-game-is.preview: **Glass Empires** is a role-playing game that simulates **pre-industrial human power dynamics** on an **animistic discworld** where there is **no distinction between magic & nature**.

/lore/history.title: History of Glass
/lore/history.preview: **The Blooming** cut the prior Age short & filled the world with jungle & ruin. The old kings of the world, the **Sleepers**, hid their servants along with themselves in great underground vaults ...…

/lore/island-of-glass/architecture.title: Architecture
/lore/island-of-glass/architecture.preview: Glass builds in stone & timber, shifting the ratio with the climate.

/lore/island-of-glass/geography-geodesti.title: Geography & Biome
/lore/island-of-glass/geography-geodesti.preview: Glass is largely *temperate continental* to *humid subtropical* with a rimward tendency to boreal/taiga features on the Arcades (the rimward coast, whose coastal pillars form stone arcades).

/lore/island-of-glass/wonders.title: Wonders of Glass
/lore/island-of-glass/wonders.preview: Glass is peppered with a handful of strategically significant wonders.

/lore/island-of-glass.title: The Island of Glass
/lore/island-of-glass.preview: Glass is an island roughly half the size of Honshu, triangular in shape, with a long mountain range on its counterclockwise side and a smaller cluster of peaks to its clockwise point. The land between…

/lore/scenario/commons-revolt.title: Commons Revolt
/lore/scenario/commons-revolt.preview: **Everything went to hell the instant Kain abdicated his throne.** There was five years of "peace" between the *Autumn Massacre* of the Imperial court and the outbreak of the Coraler invasion, & even…

/lore/scenario/glass-coralers.title: Glass Coralers
/lore/scenario/glass-coralers.preview: **It's not just Glass that's burning.** Plague of the reef, economic strangulation, & civil war has swept the Inner Sea. Sixteen hundred years of *total* domination over the waves where no man nor…

/lore/scenario/house-arcades.title: House Arcades
/lore/scenario/house-arcades.preview: **You are the son of a line of fore-fathers going back to the earliest age of Glassian history**, and this war has gone on long enough. It's an embarrassment to your blood. An initial effort to revive…

/lore/scenario/house-beacon.title: House Beacon
/lore/scenario/house-beacon.preview: You are the heir of a Lord who lost his original lands & home to a rampaging native god, and while your father may have forgiven it, you have not. The Hydrean Jesuits discovered this, and, in secret,…

/lore/scenario/house-eagle.title: House Eagle
/lore/scenario/house-eagle.preview: "Protected" from the civil war by virtue of your trade with the **Venetian League's merchants**, you are a **cosmopolitan opportunistic warmonger** bathed in banking influence from a young age. You…

/lore/scenario/imperial-family.title: Imperial Family
/lore/scenario/imperial-family.preview: The possessed madwoman regent and the 6-year-old heir — a dying dynasty with the only legitimate claim to the throne.

/lore/scenario/minor-powers.title: Minor Powers
/lore/scenario/minor-powers.preview: **Not everyone on Glass is fighting for dominance.** Some are just looking to survive. Some have grand ideals. Some simply don't have the means to conquer ... yet.

/lore/scenario/oropolis-and-kain.title: Oropolis & Kain
/lore/scenario/oropolis-and-kain.preview: The Golden God-Emperor. The Second Phoenix. The Absent King. Here he's hid for 20 years, cloistered from his children in a vast temple-mountain of every wonder & pleasure. Who can breach his fortress?

/lore/scenario.title: The Scenario
/lore/scenario.preview: **The main goal of every major Faction is to conquer Glass**. In order to conquer Glass, *one* of the two following states must be achieved:

/lore/society/glassian-identity.title: Glassian Identity
/lore/society/glassian-identity.preview: Glassians are isolationist xenophobic people who believe that they are unique amongst all the people on Aia in that they are immortal. They are reborn after death, like Kain, who is reborn very…

/lore/society/religion-and-gods.title: Religion & Gods
/lore/society/religion-and-gods.preview: Aia is home to "an ecosystem of gods." Everyone maintains a basic relationship to the gods that matter most to them — rice, water, war, threshold, ancestral spirits. There's no need to give a +1 for…

/lore/society.title: Society & Culture
/lore/society.preview: Glassians believe they are unique among all people on Aia: they are immortal, reborn after death like their god-emperor Kain. They are also porcelain-white, slender & tall, and deeply proud of their…

/lore/the-moon/getting-there-and-back.title: Getting There & Back
/lore/the-moon/getting-there-and-back.preview: Four ways to reach the Moon and two ways to return — none of them easy.

/lore/the-moon/lunar-landscape.title: Lunar Landscape
/lore/the-moon/lunar-landscape.preview: Aia's Moon is a strange & unpleasant landscape for anything not born there.

/lore/the-moon/lunarian-kingdom.title: Lunarian Kingdom
/lore/the-moon/lunarian-kingdom.preview: *Ludicrously* elitist towards anyone on the surface world. Absolutely not better than them in any way, except that they actually are. Lunarians are beautiful, intelligent, highly developed, mostly…

/lore/the-moon/practical-role.title: Practical Role
/lore/the-moon/practical-role.preview: The Lunarians have a stockpile of enormously powerful & dangerous magical weaponry & utilities that could turn the Grand Disc on its head if even a handful were brought back to the surface. Going to…

/lore/the-moon.title: The Moon
/lore/the-moon.preview: Aia's Moon is ~300 miles across, perpetually cloud-shrouded, and home to an immortal kingdom that would rather you never visit. Getting there is a heist. Being there is a horror. Coming back is a…

/lore/world-of-aia/elements-spirits.title: Elements & Spirits
/lore/world-of-aia/elements-spirits.preview: A magician will usually know most of what's contained in this section.

/lore/world-of-aia/metals-materials.title: Metals & Materials
/lore/world-of-aia/metals-materials.preview: Metal is not elemental. There are 8 metals: Lead, tin, copper, iron, mercury, silver, gold, & bismuth. Similarly, there are an enormous variety of gemstones, the vast majority of which are associated…

/lore/world-of-aia.title: The World of Aia
/lore/world-of-aia.preview: **Aia** is a **spinning flat disc** approximating **2,000 miles in diameter** ringed by a **tremendous swirling blizzard** upon its rim which sits atop a vast ocean of heavy liquid called **the…

/gm-guide/campaigns.title: Campaigns
/gm-guide/campaigns.preview: How to minimally start a game. The complete worldgen pipeline: region generation, settlement placement, fortress placement, ruins & lairs, resource distribution, faction allegiance mapping, starting…

/gm-guide/encounters-and-locations.title: Encounters & Locations
/gm-guide/encounters-and-locations.preview: You should create a 1d6 random table of at least 6 distinct Locations for each distinct area, biome, or region in play. This table is used for small, hidden/secretive, or outwardly unremarkable…

/gm-guide/running-a-braunstein.title: Running a Braunstein
/gm-guide/running-a-braunstein.preview: Glass Empires is built to be managed either as a traditional RPG campaign featuring a party of Players whose Party is based on the premise of working together to achieve a larger goal set out at the…

/conditions/encumbered.title: Encumbered
/conditions/encumbered.preview: Halves effective Speed for long-distance travel; −1 penalty on dexterity/agility Saves.

/conditions/grappled.title: Grappled
/conditions/grappled.preview: Locked in close combat; neither grappler can move except by Shoving.

/conditions/incapacitated.title: Incapacitated
/conditions/incapacitated.preview: Unconscious and at risk of death; makes a Mortality Save each turn.

/conditions/panicked.title: Panicked
/conditions/panicked.preview: Fleeing in terror; doubled damage taken from all sources.

/conditions/prone.title: Prone
/conditions/prone.preview: On the ground; quartered Speed and height. Counts as a Fact where relevant.

/glossary/battle-rating.title: Battle Rating
/glossary/battle-rating.preview: A creature's or unit's relative combat effectiveness, used for army calculations.

/glossary/glossary-actor.title: Actor
/glossary/glossary-actor.preview: An entity capable of taking action in the game world — the umbrella term for Characters, Factions, and everything that acts.

/glossary/glossary-armor.title: Armor
/glossary/glossary-armor.preview: A score (usually 1–4) granted by equipment or Features that generates Armor Points as a backup HP pool.

/glossary/glossary-calendar.title: Calendar
/glossary/glossary-calendar.preview: A timekeeping system required to track Deadlines for Moves and synchronize Players with the world state.

/glossary/glossary-campaign.title: Campaign
/glossary/glossary-campaign.preview: The framework of play, consisting of a calendar and a map depicting the region in which the game takes place.

/glossary/glossary-damage-types.title: Damage Types
/glossary/glossary-damage-types.preview: The seven categories of Damage — Acid, Electric, Ice, Fire, Mind, Physical, and Poison — that determine resistance, weakness, and immunity interactions.

/glossary/glossary-damage.title: Damage
/glossary/glossary-damage.preview: HP loss from hostile sources — 1 HP per point of Damage. May have one or more damage Types.

/glossary/glossary-fact.title: Fact
/glossary/glossary-fact.preview: A true statement about a character granting or denying permission to attempt actions.

/glossary/glossary-faction.title: Faction
/glossary/glossary-faction.preview: A statistical grouping of many characters taking collective action, led by a Leader.

/glossary/glossary-hit-dice.title: Hit Dice
/glossary/glossary-hit-dice.preview: A measure of a creature's raw toughness and combat capability — HD determines HP, contributes to Power, and scales many Features.

/glossary/glossary-level.title: Level
/glossary/glossary-level.preview: A measure of a character's or Faction's growth in power, granting access to Class Features and scaling Hit Dice.

/glossary/glossary-location.title: Location
/glossary/glossary-location.preview: A point on the campaign map representing a notable feature — settlement, resource, dungeon, or landmark.

/glossary/glossary-map.title: Map
/glossary/glossary-map.preview: The geographic area in which play happens, marked with Locations, Roads, and Terrain types — any scale measured in miles is acceptable.

/glossary/glossary-max-load.title: Max. Load and Stone
/glossary/glossary-max-load.preview: Carrying capacity measured in stone (#) — 10 lb = 1 stone. Max. Load determines encumbrance penalties.

/glossary/glossary-mood.title: Mood
/glossary/glossary-mood.preview: NPC disposition — Friendly, Neutral, or Hostile — determining baseline social interactions.

/glossary/glossary-move.title: Move
/glossary/glossary-move.preview: Any action taken in-game by an actor; may be instant or extended with a Deadline.

/glossary/glossary-power.title: Power
/glossary/glossary-power.preview: A derived stat equal to a character's Hit Dice plus their Level (HD + Level).

/glossary/glossary-resistance.title: Resistance, Weakness and Immunity
/glossary/glossary-resistance.preview: Damage modifiers: Resistance halves, Weakness doubles, and Immunity neutralizes Damage of a given Type.

/glossary/glossary-resource.title: Resource
/glossary/glossary-resource.preview: A Location that, when settled, produces trade goods of strategic significance — production bases worth fighting over.

/glossary/glossary-save.title: Save
/glossary/glossary-save.preview: A standardized 2d6 dice roll determining the outcome of uncertain, meaningful actions.

/glossary/glossary-settlement.title: Settlement
/glossary/glossary-settlement.preview: A Location permanently inhabited by one or more creatures operating a society — at least 1 Family must live there.

/glossary/glossary-silver-piece.title: Silver Piece
/glossary/glossary-silver-piece.preview: The smallest denomination of coin in circulation in Aia, representing one day of unskilled labor.

/glossary/glossary-structural-hardness.title: Structural Hardness
/glossary/glossary-structural-hardness.preview: The material hardness scale for structures — weapons of inferior hardness have their damage divided by 10 against fortifications.

/glossary/glossary-structural-hit-points.title: Structural Hit Points
/glossary/glossary-structural-hit-points.preview: A measure of a Location's fortification troop capacity combined with its resilience to assault.

/bestiary/giant-ant.title: Giant Ant
/bestiary/giant-ant.preview: Dog-sized six-legged insects organized into vast colonies ruled by mad queens and scheming princes.

/bestiary/giant-spider.title: Giant Spider
/bestiary/giant-spider.preview: Wolf-sized spider with thick limbs and leathery skin — scratchy hair, beady eyes dotting a fanged face.

/bestiary/giant-toad.title: Giant Toad
/bestiary/giant-toad.preview: Boulder-sized warty amphibian of mottled grey-green hide with bulging eyes, deceptively quick tongue, and comically unassuming appearance.

/bestiary/horse.title: Horse
/bestiary/horse.preview: Long legs, alert ears. Glossy coat, nervous eyes. Snorts and whinnies when alarmed.

/bestiary/jiangshi.title: Jiangshi
/bestiary/jiangshi.preview: A stiff, jittery pale-greenish corpse dressed in silken Magistrate's garments, dripping mercury. Dead-eyed slasher smile. Metallic sheen to its hair.

/bestiary/monkey.title: Monkey
/bestiary/monkey.preview: Ugly 3-foot-tall white/pale red-faced monkey with a tail. Horse-like grin with powerful jaws. Devious, brilliant schemer.

/bestiary/moon-rabbit-operator.title: Moon Rabbit Operator
/bestiary/moon-rabbit-operator.preview: A rabbit-eared Lunar soldier deployed to Aia on covert missions — literal-minded, well-armed, and adorably incompetent.

/bestiary/moon-rabbit.title: Moon Rabbit
/bestiary/moon-rabbit.preview: A neotenous human with rabbit ears, friendly and eternally bored — the civilian population of the Lunar Kingdom.

/bestiary/porcelain-doll.title: Porcelain Doll
/bestiary/porcelain-doll.preview: Animated porcelain doll-servants of fallen Lords, still performing their dead masters' routines.

/bestiary/skittering-horde.title: Skittering Horde
/bestiary/skittering-horde.preview: A seething carpet of countless tiny bodies — chittering, rustling, overwhelming.

/bestiary/spider-lily.title: Spider Lily
/bestiary/spider-lily.preview: Pale red egg-body on ten spindly legs, with a thick brown proboscis. Silent skitter. Faint sweet stink. Where they gather, the world of the dead draws close.

/bestiary/true-immortal.title: True Immortal
/bestiary/true-immortal.preview: An immortal human of inscrutable age; radiant, hyper-real, and probably in disguise.

/bestiary/vegetable-sprite.title: Vegetable Sprite
/bestiary/vegetable-sprite.preview: Uprooted vegetables with rootlike limbs and no faces — pattering feet and cracking stems.

/bestiary/wolf.title: Wolf
/bestiary/wolf.preview: White and rust-red fur for Birchwood camouflage. Sleek, stalking. Yips, barks, and eerie howls.

/moves/move-attack.title: Attack
/moves/move-attack.preview: Deal damage to a target in melee or ranged.

/moves/move-foresight.title: Foresight
/moves/move-foresight.preview: Assert that your character planned ahead for a challenge and make a Save to confirm.

/moves/move-hunting.title: Hunting
/moves/move-hunting.preview: Search for a specific creature in a biome where it's known to exist, with a Deadline based on the creature's Rarity Tier and a Hunting Save to determine the outcome.

/moves/move-negotiation.title: Negotiation
/moves/move-negotiation.preview: Bargain with a target — intimidation, relationship-building, seduction, and salesmanship are all negotiation.

/moves/move-persuasion.title: Persuasion
/moves/move-persuasion.preview: Convince a target that something is true, regardless of whether it actually is.

/moves/move-rest.title: Resting
/moves/move-rest.preview: Heal HP and restore AP by remaining idle for an extended period.

/moves/move-travel.title: Travel
/moves/move-travel.preview: Resolve overland travel by a Party or mobile Faction across the campaign map, with Exhaustion Saves, Discoveries, and Travel Saves.

/spirits/glass-wisp.title: Glass Wisp
/spirits/glass-wisp.preview: Ambient spirit of shattered or molten glass; faint luminescence, no will of its own.
